purpose: Preserve a coherent, evidence-first visual and information system for complex repository README work.

layout.content_width: GitHub default content width; verify a narrow viewport too.
layout.hierarchy: One H1, action-oriented H2 sections, short lead paragraphs, tables only for repeated choices, and release evidence in a managed block.
layout.theme: Evidence-first technical editorial: restrained, readable, and project-native rather than dashboard-like.

component_rules: Use Markdown for claims, commands, links, and accessibility text.
Do not add counters, trackers, profile widgets, remote fonts, or unverified badges for decoration.
Use PNG/JPG/WebP for all reader-facing generated or structural visual delivery; retain SVG or Mermaid only as internal editable sources, never as a Markdown display target.
Re-use approved colors, whitespace, card-like geometry, and visual hierarchy before adding a new treatment.

in_image_text.decision_rule: Use concise in-image labels, legends, or numbered callouts only when they materially improve a workflow, architecture, comparison, or decision explanation. Keep conceptual hero artwork text-free when labels would not improve comprehension.
in_image_text.typography: Use the approved navy, teal, amber, and canvas palette; one restrained sans-serif hierarchy; consistent title, label, and caption sizes; generous whitespace; and high contrast at GitHub content width and narrow width.
in_image_text.language_policy: Use one primary language per image. Put bilingual detail, commands, and accessibility explanations in Markdown captions unless a reviewed bilingual visual is materially clearer.
quality_gate: Verify exact displayed wording, readability, contrast, and clipping at delivery size.
Regenerate images with garbled, inconsistent, unsupported, or low-contrast text.
Record whether the asset is text-free, labeled, or uses a legend in its provenance.

collaboration_loop_labeled.role: Labeled reader-facing collaboration role and feedback-loop explanation.
collaboration_loop_labeled.regenerate_when: The role model, in-image labels, or shared typography and palette contract changes materially.

architecture_overview.regenerate_when: The architecture graph or owner relationships change materially.

file_organization.regenerate_when: The transactional organization or rollback topology changes materially.

maintenance.complexity_threshold: Whole README, six or more headings, three or more top-level sections changed, or more than one visual changed.
required_checks: design-system manifest update when visual decisions change

user_facing_explanation_policy.route: Evaluate every changed main user-facing explanation with github-readme-presentation. Use README mode for complex reader journeys and Audit mode otherwise.
